topic: Re-evaluate the telepty architecture based on the user`s strict boundaries: 1. A session equals a physical, visible terminal window. If the window closes, the session MUST die. No zombies. 2. Background persistence is NOT telepty`s job (users can use tmux if they want, but telepty doesn`t manage it). 3. Unexpected disconnects (network drop, sleep) are treated as session death, and recovery is handled via a higher-level `Handoff` of context to a new session, NOT by keeping zombie processes alive. Double-check this philosophy for flaws, focusing on how AI agents will communicate (Pub/Sub) within this strict ephemeral foreground model.
